title: Availability, Disruption Budgets, Graceful Draining, Autoscaling, and Capacity · Object and Event Forensics
summary: An incident responder inherits a plausible but defective availability and autoscaling operating policy; preserve status and events, isolate the first causal fault in availability, disruption budgets, graceful draining, autoscaling, and capacity, and retain a regression check. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in availability and autoscaling operating policy and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, chan…
objectives: Translate service objectives into replica count, failure domains, topology placement, readiness, surge capacity, dependency limits, and recovery evidence.
Calculate minAvailable or maxUnavailable, unhealthy eviction policy, controller scale, simultaneous drains, and voluntary versus involuntary disruption limits.
Derive HPA desired replicas from requests, utilization, custom or external metrics, missing samples, tolerance, stabilization, and scale policies.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug K8s Replica Failure Domain Capacity
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug service has 5 replicas but all share one zone, a strict disruption budget blocks drain, and HPA oscillates because requests and metrics are wrong. Balance failure domains, PDB, rollout surge, autoscaling signals, stabilization, and capacity. Competency probe: Translate service objectives into replica count, failure domains, topology placement, readiness, surge capacity, dependency limits, and recovery evidence.

Inspect the K8s Replica Failure Domain Capacity trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

For Inspect the failure record · Debug K8s Replica Failure Domain Capacity, keep K8s Replica Failure Domain Capacity connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug K8s Capacity Chaos Recovery Evidence
steps.instruction:
Append the Kubernetes evidence item headed "  # Evidence: k8s-capacity-chaos-recovery-evidence-1v5slv". Tailor its availability-policy.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic API or manifest review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run kubectl, Helm, kubeadm, and cluster commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug K8s Capacity Chaos Recovery Evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns K8s Capacity Chaos Recovery Evidence into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug K8s Capacity Chaos Recovery Evidence, keep K8s Capacity Chaos Recovery Evidence connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-capacity-chaos-recovery-evidence-1v5slv
  - competency: k8s-capacity-chaos-recovery-evidence
    artifact: availability-policy.yaml
    decision: capacity chaos recovery evidence
    environment: remote context with short-lived federated workload identity
    fault-injection: change a selector so one canary endpoint overlaps production
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: 1v5slv
    change: |
      objective: 99.9-percent-availability
      replicas: 4-across-2-zones
      pdb: minAvailable-3
      hpa: autoscaling-v2-cpu-and-queue
      stabilization: bounded
      node-headroom: measured
      faults: drain-zone-loss-metrics-gap
    verify: kubectl get deployment,pdb,hpa -n intake -o json
    failure: Reject replica-count-only HA, PDB as involuntary-failure protection, limit-based HPA math, or autoscalers without capacity and feedback analysis.
    repair: Tie replicas and topology to objectives, calculate disruption and scaling, reserve headroom, inject lag and failure, and measure user-visible service.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The K8s Capacity Chaos Recovery Evidence structural check is only one gate. availability and autoscaling operating policy must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

steps.title: Publish the incident record · Debug K8s Capacity Chaos Recovery Evidence
steps.instruction:
Write the debug evidence note for K8s Capacity Chaos Recovery Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A debug service has 3 replicas but all share one zone, a strict disruption budget blocks drain, and HPA oscillates because requests and metrics are wrong. Balance failure domains, PDB, rollout surge, autoscaling signals, stabilization, and capacity. Competency probe: Test load, Pod loss, node drain, zone loss, slow dependency, scaling lag, quota, and recovery while measuring user-visible service objectives.

Evidence target: Publish the incident record · Debug K8s Capacity Chaos Recovery Evidence.
steps.why: Retrieving K8s Capacity Chaos Recovery Evidence through the concrete availability and autoscaling operating policy case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Publish the incident record · Debug K8s Capacity Chaos Recovery Evidence, keep K8s Capacity Chaos Recovery Evidence connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test load, Pod loss, node drain, zone loss, slow dependency, scaling lag, quota, and recovery while measuring user-visible service objectives.

hints: For Publish the incident record · Debug K8s Capacity Chaos Recovery Evidence, name the context, namespace, API identity, desired state, observed status, controller, policy, and failure-domain boundary, K8s Capacity Chaos Recovery Evidence precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Publish the incident record · Debug K8s Capacity Chaos Recovery Evidence, trace this concrete input and predict admission, defaulting, scheduling, reconciliation, status, traffic, storage, policy, disruption, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A debug service has 3 replicas but all share one zone, a strict disruption budget blocks drain, and HPA oscillates because requests and metrics are wrong. Balance failure domains, PDB, rollout surge, autoscaling signals, stabilization, and capacity. Competency probe: Test load, Pod loss, node drain, zone loss, slow dependency, scaling lag, quota, and recovery while measuring user-visible service objectives.

For Publish the incident record · Debug K8s Capacity Chaos Recovery Evidence, challenge the documented misconception with object, condition, event, policy, failure, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: A successful kubectl drain and replica count prove the availability objective under real traffic.
